candidates.title_a: Organizations cannot outgrow the character and intellectual capacity of their leadership
candidates.title_b: Organizations cannot outgrow the intellectual capacity of their leadership
candidates.stance_a: The ceiling on a company's growth is determined by the founder's willingness to develop new competencies, not by market opportunity or capital
candidates.stance_b: The primary ceiling on business growth is the founder's own skill gaps, not market conditions or capital constraints.

candidates.title_a: Unpaid founder wages become leverage weapons during founder breakups
candidates.title_b: Unpaid founder wages become leverage weapons during co-founder breakups
candidates.stance_a: Paying founders minimum wage from day one is a defensive legal strategy, not a compensation decision — it eliminates leverage that departing founders can use to extract unearned equity.
candidates.stance_b: Founders who skip paying themselves minimum wage are creating a legal vulnerability that a departing co-founder will exploit for vesting acceleration.

candidates.title_a: Start with a market so small it looks worthless then expand concentrically
candidates.title_b: Start with a small market and expand concentrically — going after large markets on day one signals broken category definition
candidates.stance_a: The optimal startup market entry is a market so tiny that business school analysis would dismiss it as having no value.
candidates.stance_b: Successful monopoly-building companies start by dominating a market so small that business school analysis dismisses it as valueless, then expand in concentric circles into adjacent markets.

candidates.title_a: Children's neural plasticity enables 16x faster procedural relearning than adults
candidates.title_b: Children's neural plasticity creates a 16x speed advantage in procedural relearning over adults
candidates.stance_a: Children acquire new procedural patterns dramatically faster than adults because their neural pathways are less consolidated and more malleable
candidates.stance_b: Children can rewire procedural motor algorithms an order of magnitude faster than adults due to greater neural plasticity

candidates.title_a: Trade novelty for loyalty, exploration for trust, chase for shared journey
candidates.title_b: Trade novelty for loyalty, exploration for trust, chase for journey
candidates.stance_a: Commitment is a deliberate trade of three specific goods (novelty, exploration, chase) for three specific returns (loyalty/predictability, trust, shared purpose), and people who can't name the trade shouldn't make it.
candidates.stance_b: Long-term commitment is best understood as three explicit trades — novelty for loyalty, exploration for trust, chase for journey — and people who cannot name what they're gaining should not make the trade.

candidates.title_a: Newly formed neural pathways are fragile and lose to established pathways under any cognitive load
candidates.title_b: Fragile new neural pathways revert to old patterns under minimal cognitive load
candidates.stance_a: Recently acquired procedural skills are extremely fragile and will be instantly overridden by older established neural pathways whenever attention is divided or cognitive load increases.
candidates.stance_b: Newly acquired procedural skills exist as fragile pathways that instantly collapse back to legacy algorithms when attention is divided, even by trivial distractions like a phone vibration

candidates.title_a: Children acquire opposing motor patterns 16x faster than adults due to higher neural plasticity
candidates.title_b: Children's neural plasticity enables 16x faster procedural relearning than adults
candidates.stance_a: A child's brain can rewire procedural algorithms an order of magnitude faster than an adult's, making early childhood the critical window for foundational skill acquisition.
candidates.stance_b: Children acquire new procedural patterns dramatically faster than adults because their neural pathways are less consolidated and more malleable

candidates.title_a: Value creation and value capture are independent variables — most innovators in history captured zero
candidates.title_b: Value creation and value capture are independent variables — most innovators capture zero
candidates.stance_a: The structure of market competition matters more than the magnitude of innovation, because value creation (X) and value capture (Y%) are completely independent variables
candidates.stance_b: The amount of value a business creates for the world (X) is completely independent of the percentage it captures (Y%), and most technological and scientific breakthroughs throughout history resulted in Y=0% for their creators.

candidates.title_a: Every management decision is interpreted by the entire company, not just the person in the room
candidates.title_b: Every management decision ripples through the entire company not just the person in the room
candidates.stance_a: CEOs who evaluate decisions only from their own perspective and the affected person's perspective will produce dangerous unintended cultural consequences.
candidates.stance_b: CEOs who evaluate decisions only from their own perspective and the affected individual's perspective will consistently produce dangerous unintended consequences across the organization.

candidates.title_a: Aligning incentives to activated users instead of registrations accelerates real growth
candidates.title_b: Aligning incentives to activated users instead of registrations accelerates real growth
candidates.stance_a: Paying for activated users rather than registered users causes short-term registration drops but accelerates long-term growth by aligning acquisition channels with retention
candidates.stance_b: Changing affiliate compensation from confirmed registrations to activated users drops registration volume but accelerates engaged user growth by forcing better landing experiences.

candidates.title_b: Aligning incentives to activated users instead of registrations accelerates real growth

candidates.title_a: Search the discard bin instead of seeking novel undiscovered ideas
candidates.title_b: Search the discard bin instead of seeking novel ideas
candidates.stance_a: Find ideas that have been discarded by others rather than trying to discover something no one has ever thought of.
candidates.stance_b: The best startup ideas are ones that have already been considered and rejected by others, not ones nobody has ever thought of.

candidates.title_a: Every management decision is interpreted by the entire company not just the person in the room
candidates.title_b: Every management decision ripples through the entire company not just the person in the room

candidates.stance_b: CEOs who evaluate decisions only from their own perspective and the affected individual's perspective will consistently produce dangerous unintended consequences across the organization.

candidates.title_a: Proton gradients store energy like batteries through concentration differentials not chemical bonds
candidates.title_b: Proton gradient batteries: life stores energy through concentration differentials, not chemical bonds alone
candidates.stance_a: Biological energy storage fundamentally works by creating concentration imbalances across membranes, not by storing energy in individual molecules.
candidates.stance_b: Biological energy storage fundamentally relies on maintaining concentration gradients across membranes, making membrane integrity as critical as fuel availability

candidates.title_a: Romantic love shares neurochemical pathways with addiction and OCD
candidates.title_b: Romantic love recruits the same neural circuitry as addiction and OCD
candidates.stance_a: The neurochemical profile of romantic love — elevated dopamine and altered serotonin — is functionally identical to addiction and obsessive-compulsive disorder, making early-stage love a predictable cognitive impairment rather than a higher state.
candidates.stance_b: Romantic love is neurochemically indistinguishable from addiction and shares obsessive-rumination pathways with OCD, making it a temporary altered state rather than a reliable signal of compatibility.

candidates.title_a: Disproportionate equity splits are absent from every top YC company
candidates.title_b: Disproportionate equity splits are absent from all top YC companies
candidates.stance_a: Equal or near-equal founder equity splits are a necessary condition for reaching top-tier startup outcomes, not merely a nice-to-have.
candidates.stance_b: Equal or near-equal founder equity splits are a necessary condition for startup success, not merely a nice-to-have preference.

candidates.title_b: Aligning incentives to activated users instead of registrations accelerates real growth

candidates.stance_b: Changing affiliate compensation from confirmed registrations to activated users drops registration volume but accelerates engaged user growth by forcing better landing experiences.

candidates.title_a: Human intuition linearizes exponential processes causing systematic underestimation
candidates.title_b: Human intuition linearizes exponential growth creating systematic underestimation
candidates.stance_a: Humans are neurologically incapable of intuitively grasping exponential growth and will consistently underestimate its speed by defaulting to linear mental models.
candidates.stance_b: People consistently underestimate exponential processes because the brain defaults to linear extrapolation from recent data points, making early-stage exponentials look deceptively manageable.

candidates.title_b: Aligning incentives to activated users instead of registrations accelerates real growth
candidates.stance_a: Paying for deeper user actions rather than surface registrations causes short-term metric drops but dramatically accelerates genuine growth by aligning incentive structures with actual value creation.
candidates.stance_b: Changing affiliate compensation from confirmed registrations to activated users drops registration volume but accelerates engaged user growth by forcing better landing experiences.

candidates.title_a: Hardware product development requires explicit systems thinking across hardware software and data as equal pillars
candidates.title_b: A 1% decrease in churn produces the same growth effect as a 1% increase in conversion but is far cheaper to achieve
candidates.stance_a: Companies building connected products must treat hardware, software, and data as three equal pillars rather than letting one discipline dominate, because the product experience emerges from their intersection.
candidates.stance_b: Most startups over-invest in top-of-funnel conversion optimization while neglecting churn reduction, despite churn reduction being cheaper, easier, and mathematically equivalent in growth impact

candidates.title_a: Lock-free event queues prevent thread contention in high-throughput pipelines
candidates.title_b: Lock-free architectures outperform thread-safe ones in real-time event pipelines
candidates.stance_a: Lock-free data structures are essential for high-throughput event-driven systems because even microsecond-level lock contention destroys latency guarantees.
candidates.stance_b: Lock-free data structures and event queues are not just an optimization but a requirement for real-time systems — even brief mutex contention creates unacceptable latency spikes that invalidate time-sensitive decisions.

candidates.title_a: Retention curve asymptoting parallel to x-axis is the binary test for product-market fit
candidates.title_b: Retention curve asymptoting parallel to x-axis is the definitive product-market fit test
candidates.stance_a: If your cohort retention curve does not flatten to a horizontal asymptote, no amount of growth tactics or virality will save the business.
candidates.stance_b: A product has viable product-market fit if and only if its cohort retention curve flattens to a horizontal asymptote rather than declining to zero

candidates.title_a: Aligning with a powerful system's needs is the strongest evolutionary survival strategy
candidates.title_b: Being useful to dominant players is the strongest evolutionary survival strategy
candidates.stance_a: Species (and companies) that make themselves useful to dominant systems achieve population scale that independent optimization never could.
candidates.stance_b: Species that became useful to humans thrived more than those that were independently superior — the same applies to products in platform ecosystems

candidates.title_a: Ignorance as fuel: science advances from the boundary of what is unknown, not from accumulated facts
candidates.title_b: Ignorance is the fuel of science, not its enemy
candidates.stance_a: The primary driver of scientific and intellectual progress is productive ignorance — the quality of questions at the knowledge frontier — not the accumulation of known facts.
candidates.stance_b: Scientific progress is driven primarily by the expanding frontier of what we don't know rather than by the accumulation of what we do know — facts are byproducts, not the engine.

candidates.title_a: API versioning preserves backward compatibility while enabling evolution
candidates.title_b: API versioning through URL paths prevents breaking existing clients during evolution
candidates.stance_a: Breaking API changes without versioning destroys client trust and creates cascading failures, making explicit version management a non-negotiable practice for any API with external consumers.
candidates.stance_b: Backward-compatible API evolution through explicit versioning (v1/v2 paths for REST, additive field changes for GraphQL) is more reliable than in-place modifications that risk breaking existing consumers

candidates.title_a: Become a cog in the industry before trying to disrupt it
candidates.title_b: Become a cog in the industry before trying to disrupt it
candidates.stance_a: Founders who temporarily work inside the industry they want to disrupt discover exploitable inefficiencies that pure outsiders miss.
candidates.stance_b: Founders who temporarily work as employees in their target industry discover inefficiencies that outsiders cannot see, giving them a structural advantage over both incumbents and other startups.

candidates.title_a: The abstract data type and implementation split is the foundational design pattern behind all robust system architecture
candidates.title_b: The abstraction-implementation gap is the key to mastering any complex system
candidates.stance_a: Separating interface contracts from implementation details is not just good practice — it is the single pattern that makes systems evolvable and prevents architectural lock-in.
candidates.stance_b: Understanding the separation between what a system promises (interface) and how it delivers (implementation) is the single most important mental model for navigating complexity in any domain

candidates.title_a: Series A now requires billion-dollar funds because round sizes and follow-on needs have exploded
candidates.title_b: The BPO-to-AI transition is a $400B market that dwarfs SaaS displacement
candidates.stance_a: The venture capital industry has bifurcated — billion-dollar funds are necessary to lead Series A rounds because companies stay private longer, round sizes have grown dramatically, and smaller funds cannot provide adequate follow-on capital.
candidates.stance_b: AI replacing BPO services in financial services alone represents a $400B annual market — roughly equal to all global cloud software — and creates fundamentally better economics because it transforms 10-15% margin services into 80% margin AI businesses.

candidates.title_a: Replace free trials with annual contracts plus a 30-day opt-out clause
candidates.title_b: A small disciplined faction can capture a large disorganized system through decisive action at key nodes
candidates.stance_a: Free trials give startups zero commitment, zero validation, and zero revenue, while an annual contract with a 30-day cancellation clause achieves the same risk reduction for the buyer while preserving real commitment for the seller.
candidates.stance_b: Organizational or political power is concentrated in a few critical nodes (communications, transportation, financial systems), and a small group that seizes those nodes controls the entire system.

candidates.title_a: Single-city economies create fragility masked by aggregate statistics
candidates.title_b: The practical nutrition hierarchy: eliminate processed food before counting any calories or macros
candidates.stance_a: When a nation's economic growth concentrates in one city (London), aggregate GDP figures mask the structural decline of everywhere else, creating a politically unstable dual economy.
candidates.stance_b: The single highest-leverage nutrition intervention is eliminating ultra-processed foods (the one-ingredient rule), which automatically resolves most dietary problems before any calorie counting, macro tracking, or supplementation becomes relevant.

candidates.title_a: Feeding disinformation to overconfident adversaries exploits their confirmation bias
candidates.title_b: Therapeutic phlebotomy removes accumulated heavy metals and stored toxins from blood
candidates.stance_a: Opponents who believe they are winning will readily accept intelligence that confirms their assumption, making them highly vulnerable to strategic deception.
candidates.stance_b: Regular blood donation or therapeutic phlebotomy can reduce accumulated heavy metals, stored seed oil metabolites, and excess iron that contribute to oxidative stress

candidates.title_a: Automation that transfers labor to consumers fails when the consumer effort exceeds the convenience benefit
candidates.title_b: Elimination diet is the most powerful diagnostic tool for autoimmune conditions
candidates.stance_a: Self-checkout automation failed to replace cashiers because it transferred labor to consumers without providing sufficient convenience benefit to compensate for the effort.
candidates.stance_b: A 3-4 week elimination diet starting with meat and fruit is more diagnostically valuable than any medical test for identifying autoimmune food triggers

candidates.title_a: Control of natural resources creates geopolitical power that transcends economic efficiency arguments
candidates.title_b: Menstrual cramps respond to magnesium, vitamin D, omega-3, and anti-inflammatory dietary changes
candidates.stance_a: Nations and companies that control essential natural resources wield power disproportionate to the resources economic value because the resources are necessary for all other economic activity.
candidates.stance_b: Dysmenorrhea is driven by excess prostaglandins from inflammation; magnesium (relaxes smooth muscle), vitamin D (modulates inflammation), omega-3s (compete with pro-inflammatory omega-6), and reducing processed food address root causes

candidates.title_a: CEO leverage comes from product vision and team communication
candidates.title_b: Innovation happens in the bathtub not at the desk
candidates.stance_a: A CEO's two highest-leverage activities are setting the right 5-10 year product vision based on technology trajectory and communicating that vision clearly to the team.
candidates.stance_b: The most creative ideas emerge during rest and unfocused time, and AI's greatest value is enabling people to capture and develop ideas during these 'bed, bus, and bathtub' moments rather than forcing structured desk work.

candidates.title_a: De-risk FOMU with warranties, professional services, peer introductions, and land-with-expand plans
candidates.title_b: With AI products you must ship to learn because you cannot pre-test all capabilities
candidates.stance_a: In a FOMU-driven market, the seller's job shifts from creating urgency to removing risk — through concrete mechanisms like warranties, bundled professional services, trusted peer introductions, and deliberate land-and-expand strategies.
candidates.stance_b: AI products powered by large models have effectively infinite use cases that cannot be pre-tested, making rapid shipping and learning from real user behavior the only viable product development strategy.

candidates.title_a: AI's usage gap between hype and habit represents both risk and opportunity
candidates.title_b: Product customization creates the illusion of self-expression while driving higher average order value
candidates.stance_a: AI applications have impressive topline metrics but still show poor daily engagement and retention compared to mobile apps, meaning the gap between demo and daily habit remains the key product challenge.
candidates.stance_b: Offering extensive customization options makes customers feel they are expressing their identity while systematically increasing the price of every transaction.

candidates.title_a: The Frankenstein trap: building every user request hides the real problem
candidates.title_b: User feature requests mask underlying problems — dig for the real need
candidates.stance_a: Implementing feature requests at face value creates a Frankenstein product that obscures the underlying user problem, while digging into why users request features reveals the actual issue to solve.
candidates.stance_b: Building features users explicitly request creates Frankenstein products; the real value comes from identifying the underlying problem behind each request.

candidates.title_a: Human intuition linearizes exponential processes creating systematic prediction failure
candidates.title_b: Human intuition linearizes exponential growth creating systematic underestimation
candidates.stance_a: People consistently underestimate exponential growth because the brain defaults to linear extrapolation, making every exponential trend feel surprising until it is too late to act.
candidates.stance_b: People consistently underestimate exponential processes because the brain defaults to linear extrapolation from recent data points, making early-stage exponentials look deceptively manageable.

candidates.title_a: Byproducts can become more valuable than the intended product
candidates.title_b: Toxic byproducts can become the primary value proposition
candidates.stance_a: The most transformative outputs of a system are often unintended side effects rather than the designed primary output
candidates.stance_b: The most valuable output of a system is often an unintended byproduct rather than the designed product, because byproducts serve adjacent ecosystems the original system never targeted

candidates.title_a: Cells store energy as concentration gradients, not just chemical bonds
candidates.title_b: Concentration gradients are biology's universal energy storage mechanism
candidates.stance_a: Proton gradients across membranes function as rechargeable batteries and represent a fundamental energy storage mechanism distinct from chemical bond energy.
candidates.stance_b: Life stores energy primarily through chemical concentration imbalances across membranes rather than through direct chemical bonds, making gradient management the fundamental bioenergetic skill

candidates.title_a: Kissing evolved as a biological mate-compatibility test
candidates.title_b: Kissing evolved as a biological mate-selection taste test via saliva hormones
candidates.stance_a: Kissing persists in humans primarily because saliva exchange transmits hormonal and immunological data that enables subconscious mate quality assessment
candidates.stance_b: Kissing persisted evolutionarily because saliva exchange transmits testosterone and health markers that enable subconscious mate quality assessment.

candidates.title_a: Abstract data types separate interface from implementation enabling interchangeable designs
candidates.title_b: The abstraction-implementation gap is the key to mastering any complex system
candidates.stance_a: Defining what a data structure must do (interface) separately from how it does it (implementation) is the foundational design principle that enables all software flexibility and modularity.
candidates.stance_b: Understanding the separation between what a system promises (interface) and how it delivers (implementation) is the single most important mental model for navigating complexity in any domain

candidates.title_a: Human white sclera evolved specifically to broadcast gaze direction
candidates.title_b: Human white sclera evolved specifically to broadcast gaze direction for cooperation
candidates.stance_a: The distinctive white sclera of human eyes is an evolved cooperative signal that sacrifices stealth for social coordination through visible gaze direction
candidates.stance_b: The distinctive white sclera of human eyes is a cooperative adaptation that deliberately makes gaze direction visible to others, unlike predators whose eyes conceal gaze.

candidates.title_a: Value creation and value capture are independent variables
candidates.title_b: Value creation and value capture are independent variables
candidates.stance_a: A business can create enormous value for the world yet capture almost none of it, making industry structure more important than innovation quality for founder wealth.
candidates.stance_b: The amount of value a business creates for the world has no correlation with how much of that value the business captures — market structure determines capture, not contribution.

candidates.title_a: Logarithmic scales transform exponential confusion into linear clarity for pattern recognition
candidates.title_b: Logarithmic plotting transforms exponential curves into lines making growth rate changes visible
candidates.stance_a: Plotting data on a log scale is not just a visualization trick but a cognitive prosthetic that converts an unintuitive process into one humans can reason about natively
candidates.stance_b: Plotting data on a logarithmic scale converts exponential growth into straight lines, making deviations from constant-rate growth immediately visible to human perception in a way that linear-scale plots cannot.

candidates.title_a: Disproportionate equity splits are absent from every top YC company
candidates.title_b: Top YC companies have zero instances of significantly disproportionate equity splits
candidates.stance_a: Equal or near-equal founder equity splits are a necessary condition for reaching top-tier startup outcomes, not merely a nice-to-have.
candidates.stance_b: Equal or near-equal equity splits among co-founders correlate with startup success because disproportionate splits signal misaligned commitment and unresolved trust issues.

candidates.title_a: Last mover advantage matters more than first mover advantage
candidates.title_b: Last mover advantage matters more than first mover advantage
candidates.stance_a: Most of a technology company's value (75-85%) comes from cash flows 10+ years in the future, which means durability of monopoly position matters far more than growth rate or being first to market.
candidates.stance_b: Being the last company to dominate a category is far more valuable than being first — most of a tech company's value comes from cash flows 10+ years in the future, making durability more important than growth rate.

candidates.title_a: Romantic love suppresses prefrontal cortex decision-making
candidates.title_b: Romantic love deactivates prefrontal decision-making regions
candidates.stance_a: Early-stage romantic love systematically impairs rational judgment by deactivating brain regions responsible for planning and decision-making, not just emotionally but neurologically.
candidates.stance_b: Intense romantic love systematically impairs rational judgment by shutting down the brain's planning and decision-making centers, making it a predictable cognitive vulnerability rather than mere foolishness.

candidates.title_a: Last mover advantage dominates first mover advantage because 75-85% of company value comes from cash flows a decade out
candidates.title_b: Last mover advantage matters more than first mover advantage
candidates.stance_a: Being the last mover in a category — the company that defines the definitive version — matters far more than being first, because the vast majority of value exists in the distant future
candidates.stance_b: Being the last dominant company in a category is far more valuable than being the first entrant, because 75-85% of a tech company's value comes from cash flows a decade or more in the future.

candidates.title_a: Exploit asymmetries incumbents cannot match due to structural economic or technical constraints
candidates.title_b: Startups win enterprise markets by exploiting asymmetries incumbents structurally cannot match
candidates.stance_a: The most durable competitive advantages come from doing things incumbents structurally cannot do — not things they choose not to do
candidates.stance_b: The most durable enterprise startup advantages come from doing things that are economically or technically infeasible for incumbents, not from building better features in the same model.
